faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our practice and services.

q1.question: Do you accept insurance?
q1.answer: Yes, we accept most major insurance plans including Blue Cross Blue Shield, Aetna, UnitedHealthcare, Cigna, Medicare, and Humana. Please contact our office to verify your specific plan coverage before your visit.

q2.question: How do I schedule an appointment?
q2.answer: You can schedule an appointment by calling our office, using our online booking form, or messaging us on WhatsApp. We typically respond within 24 hours to confirm your appointment.

q3.question: What should I bring to my first visit?
q3.answer: Please bring a valid photo ID, insurance card, referral letter (if required), list of current medications, and any relevant imaging studies. Arrive 15 minutes early to complete necessary paperwork.

q4.question: How long is a typical consultation?
q4.answer: Initial consultations typically last 30-45 minutes. This includes a thorough examination, review of imaging, discussion of diagnosis, and a detailed treatment plan. Follow-up visits are usually 15-20 minutes.

q5.question: Do you offer emergency services?
q5.answer: While we do not operate an emergency room, we prioritize urgent orthopedic cases and can often see patients within 24-48 hours. For life-threatening emergencies, please call 911.

q6.question: What is the cancellation policy?
q6.answer: We require 24 hours notice for appointment cancellations. Late cancellations or no-shows may be subject to a fee. We understand emergencies happen and handle each situation individually.

q7.question: Do you offer virtual consultations?
q7.answer: Yes, we offer telemedicine consultations for follow-up visits, second opinions, and initial screening. Virtual visits are conducted through a secure, HIPAA-compliant platform.

q8.question: What is the recovery time for common procedures?
q8.answer: Recovery varies by procedure. Arthroscopic surgery: 1-6 weeks. Joint replacement: 3-6 months. Fracture healing: 6-12 weeks. We provide detailed recovery timelines during your consultation based on your specific treatment plan.
